subject: Anthropology
title: Ranked Societies
description: A quick win: understand Ranked Societies.

hook.text: Everyone has a place. Chiefs sit higher, commoners sit lower. Rank is inherited, and moving up is nearly impossible.

buildup.text: Ranked societies give unequal prestige to lineages but limit access to wealth less strictly than stratified ones.

discovery.text: Polynesian chiefdoms are classic examples. Birth order and genealogy determine your rank in the social hierarchy.

twist.text: High rank brings duties, not just privileges. Chiefs must redistribute wealth or lose respect and legitimacy.

climax.text: Ranked societies sit between equality and stratification. Prestige is unequal, but extreme wealth gaps are limited.

punchline.text: Rank exists, but hoarding is still forbidden.

quiz.question: What distinguishes ranked from egalitarian societies?
options: Lineages have unequal prestige but extreme wealth gaps are limited
Everyone is completely equal in ranked societies
